task: I’ve got a short notice in front of me and I want to make sure I’m not missing anything important. Can you help me break it down into the main points and what I should do next?

turns.agent_response:
Yes — paste the notice text or upload a photo, and I’ll break it into:

- **main point**
- **what it requires**
- **deadline / timing**
- **what to do next**
- **what is just filler**

If you want, I can keep it to **3 bullets max**.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah. Here it is, plain enough:

NOTICE OF RENT INCREASE
Effective March 1, 2026, your monthly rent will increase from $1,425 to $1,525.
This notice is provided in accordance with local and state requirements.
If you have questions, contact the management office during business hours.

That’s the whole thing. Don’t dress it up.

turns.agent_response:
Main point:
- Your rent is going up.

What it requires:
- Pay **$1,525/month** starting **March 1, 2026**.

What to do next:
- Check whether the notice timing is valid for your lease/local rules.
- If it looks wrong, ask management for the basis in writing.
- Update your payment amount before March 1.

What’s just filler:
- “in accordance with local and state requirements”
- “contact the management office during business hours”

If you want, I can also turn it into a **valid / maybe invalid / needs checking** checklist.

user_simulator.text:
That’s the meat of it, yeah. But you skipped the one thing I’d care about first: whether they gave enough notice. March 1, 2026 to a $1,525 rent — fine, but I’d want the date this was served, not just the effective date.

So what I’d keep in front of me:
- rent goes from $1,425 to $1,525
- starts March 1, 2026
- check the date they actually gave the notice
- if the timing is short, challenge it in writing

And I wouldn’t waste time on the office-hours line. That’s just them sounding official.

turns.agent_response:
Exactly. The key point is:

- **Rent increases from $1,425 to $1,525**
- **Effective March 1, 2026**
- **Check when the notice was actually served/given**
- **If the notice period is too short, challenge it in writing**

What matters most is the **service date**, not the “contact the office” line.

If you want, I can turn this into a **validity check** you can use on any rent notice.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah. That’s the whole point.

- rent goes from $1,425 to $1,525
- effective March 1, 2026
- the real issue is when they actually served it
- if they didn’t give enough time, hit them in writing

The office-hours line is window dressing. Nothing more.
